architecture_summary: CoAtNet is a family of hybrid models that combines depthwise convolution and self-attention via simple relative attention, and uses a vertically stacked multi-stage layout with convolution stages early and Transformer stages later. The paper adapts the C-C-T-T layout for the main CoAtNet design.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Model performance on ImageNet. 1K only denotes training on ImageNet-1K only; 21K+1K denotes pre-training on ImageNet-21K and finetuning on ImageNet-1K; PT-RA denotes applying RandAugment during 21K pre-training, and E150 means 150 epochs of 21K pre-training, which is longer than the standard 90 epochs. More results are in Appendix A.3.

novel_contributions: A family of hybrid models, CoAtNets, that marry convolution and attention for vision.
A simple relative-attention formulation that unifies depthwise convolution and self-attention with minimum additional cost.
A principled vertical layout design showing that convolution stages are better early and Transformer stages later.
State-of-the-art performance under different resource constraints across ImageNet-1K, ImageNet-21K, and JFT.

key_claims.claim: CoAtNet combines convolution and self-attention through simple relative attention and a staged architecture that stacks convolution layers before attention layers.
Without extra data, CoAtNet achieves 86.0% ImageNet top-1 accuracy and matches the prior art NFNet under similar computation and training conditions.
When pre-trained with ImageNet-21K, CoAtNet achieves 88.56% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet-1K and matches ViT-Huge pre-trained on JFT-300M while using 23x less data.
With JFT-3B pre-training, CoAtNet reaches 90.88% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet and establishes a new state-of-the-art result.
Relative attention improves generalization and transfer accuracy compared with standard attention.
The C-C-T-T layout is selected because it balances generalization, model capacity, transferability, and efficiency better than the alternative layouts.
CoAtNet-7 achieves 90.88% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet with 1.5x less computation than the prior art set by ViT-G/14.
